original_abstract: Diniyah education serves as a cornerstone of the educational landscape in Muslim-majority nations, acting as a primary vehicle for integrating formal academic standards with robust religious instruction. This article examines the historical trajectory, structural role, and modern evolution of Diniyah education policies. By embedding these religious programs into national education systems—as seen in the diverse models of Indonesia, Pakistan, and Turkey—policy initiatives have significantly bolstered religious literacy, moral development, and the formation of a cohesive religious identity. The research demonstrates that these policies improve the overall quality of formal education while deepening the spiritual and ethical grounding of society. Despite persistent implementation challenges, such as balancing religious and secular curricula or preventing radicalization, the study concludes that sustained support for Diniyah education is essential for fostering a harmonious, values-based society capable of navigating global complexities.
humanized_abstract: Diniyah education is one of the most significant components of the educational systems in the majority of the Muslim countries. Diniyah education aims at providing the formal educational standards in addition to giving a strong religious base. This paper aims at examining the history of Diniyah education policies, their structure and the development of Diniyah education policies in Indonesia, Pakistan and Turkey. The embedding of religious education in the national educational systems has resulted in enhancing the religious, moral and spiritual education of citizens and inculcating a sense of religion in them. The results of this study show that Diniyah education policy has positive effects on the educational system in the country and on the spiritual and moral development of the individuals. Hence, it is necessary to support Diniyah education in order to create a harmonious and value-based society capable of meeting the challenges of the age.
